other_observers: Robin Henry, Dick Filby; Original: Robin Henry Independently: Vic Zerbi, Art Dahl, Kendall Henry
duration: 3 observations in a 1 hour period, totaling 5 minutes
specific_location: Home of Robin and Kendall Henry, 2022 Midland Ave, Glenwood Springs, CO
description: DESCRIPTION – Robin was feeding big dishes of grape jelly to attract and photograph birds. His idea brought in a huge number of migrating birds, sometimes 50 or more Western Tanagers were in his yard, along with many Bullock’s Orioles, one 1st cycle Bullock’s x Baltimore hybrid (photographed), and this 1st cycle male Summer Tanager. By shape and size the bird was the same as the many Western Tanagers in the area. The bill was stocky and straw/gray colored like the Western Tanager’s, more streamlined than a Black-headed Grosbeak, many of these were around as well. The head, throat, nape, breast, back, and upper tail were red with many yellow feathers mixed in. The bird’s belly was yellow and the wings mostly olive-colored. The wings also had some darker and reddish feathers mainly in the secondaries, and notably no wingbars. Of note, a first cycle male Summer Tanager was seen and photographed by Ken Neubecker in Carbondale on 5/07/11 about 12 miles away. Another sighting was at the home of Vic Zerbi in Glenwood Springs on 6/01/11, about one mile away. These all could be sightings of the same individual bird.Behaviors: Repeatedly came to grape jelly at feeders and associated with Western Tanagers.Call: N/APlumage: 1st Cycle Alternate
photographer: Photographer: Robin Henry, Robin Henry, Robin Henry, Robin Henry
